div.p: William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and other worthy men of his entire land greeting. Let present and future know me to have conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to Orm son of Hugh and his heirs the abbey of Abernethy with all its just pertinents and rights justly pertaining to the same abbey of Abernethy as it was in the year and on the day that King David my grandfather was alive and dead; in wood and plain, in meadows and pastures, moors and waters, roads and paths, millponds and mills; except for 10 pounds’ worth of land that I gave to Henry Revel with the daughter of the foresaid Orm. For him and his heirs to hold of me and my heirs in feu and heritage, free and quit from all services and customs except for common aid, common army, and common work; with soke and sake, with toll and team and infangthief, yet so that he shall not have gallows nor pit except in two places, that is, at Abernethy, and at Inverarity. With 20 pounds of silver to be rendered to me and my heirs from there, that is, 10 pounds at the feast of Saint John the Baptist, and 10 pounds at the feast of Saint Brigid. I also will that his own men of Fife and Gowrie come to the pit at Abernethy; for his other lands, however, let them come to the pit at Inverarity. As witnesses: Matthew bishop of Aberdeen; Andrew bishop of Caithness; Hugh my chaplain; Earl Duncan; Earl Gilbert; Hugh Giffard; Ness son of William; Geoffrey de Malville; Walter of Berkeley; William de Mortimer; William of Lindsay. At Perth.
